title: Ambiguous-slice foundation/deep tabular residual expert
summary: A full-sequence expert has already been suggested, but a cheaper and more different view is to reserve a non-GBDT model for only the uncertain rows where tree ensembles disagree.
rationale: A full-sequence expert has already been suggested, but a cheaper and more different view is to reserve a non-GBDT model for only the uncertain rows where tree ensembles disagree. Recent tabular foundation/deep methods can be strong on moderate-size slices and often produce low-correlation errors even when they are not best globally.
implementation_hint: Define a hard slice per fold, for example rows with XGB/CatBoost rank disagreement, middle-range probabilities, or old-tyre late-race cases. On that slice only, train either TabPFN/TuneTables-style small-data expert or a compact DeepGBM-style residual model on dense numerics plus encoded cats; predict residual rank improvement relative to the base blend and only apply it on the slice, defaulting to base elsewhere.
expected_effect: Adds diversity without forcing a deep/foundation model to compete with GBDTs on all 439k rows; best case is a modest lift concentrated on ambiguous decision boundaries.
risk: Engineering complexity is higher, and some methods may not scale or may underperform badly if the selected slice is still too large/noisy.
